title: dryopteris paucijuga
intro: Dryopteris paucijuga, also known as the two-jawed wood fern, is a species of fern in the Dryopteridaceae family, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
